name: Independent Code Review
description: Changes are reviewed by an independent code reviewer prior to code being merged and released to production. Independent code review restricts the ability for anyone individual from taking a change production without involving an independent party (4-eyes principle).

attributes.text: Code Reviews are performed prior to committing a change to the main branch
testable_criteria: A code review record (e.g. pull request) exists for the change.
The code review was completed (approved) before the change was merged to the main branch.
The merge commit timestamp on main is after the approval timestamp of the code review.
No code was merged directly to main without an associated reviewed pull request.

attributes.text: Code Review approvals are performed by independent code reviewers
testable_criteria: At least one approval on the code review was granted by a reviewer other than the author of the change.
The approving reviewer did not commit or co-author any of the code changes in the pull request.
The reviewer's identity is documented in the code review record (e.g. GitHub reviewer field).
Self-approval by the change author was not used to merge the change.

attributes.text: Testing is performed in accordance with the testing policy
testable_criteria: Unit tests achieved minimum 80% code coverage for new code.
Integration tests achieved minimum 60% coverage for critical integration points.
IF the change touches security-sensitive, payment, or authentication flows THEN 100% coverage was achieved for those critical paths.
For changes to legacy code, existing coverage was maintained or improved.
Branch coverage is at least 70%.
Function coverage is at least 80%.
Coverage reports were generated and reviewed as part of the code review.
All unit tests passed with zero failures prior to release.
All integration tests passed prior to release.
End-to-end tests for critical user journeys passed prior to release.
IF applicable, performance benchmark tests passed prior to release.
Security scan results were reviewed and approved prior to release.
Tests were executed in the CI/CD pipeline before merge.
Test results are visible in the pull request status checks.
No flaky tests remained unresolved at the time of release (fixed or removed).

Pre-release checklist items (automated tests pass, coverage thresholds met, no known critical/high-severity bugs, performance tests pass if applicable, security tests pass, manual smoke tests for critical features completed) are all verified.

IF the change qualifies for a documented exception (legacy integration, third-party dependency, PoC/experimental, documentation-only, build/deployment scripts, dependency version bump, or behavior-preserving refactor) THEN the exception basis is documented and the change fits one of the listed categories.
